statement.markdown: Given a string containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. The brackets must close in the correct order, "()" and "()[]{}" are all valid but "]" and "([)]" are not.
statement.inputFormat: [string] containing a mix of valid and invalid parentheses
statement.outputFormat: [boolean] true if the input is valid, false otherwise
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n), space complexity: O(n)

hints.text: First hint text, create a map of closing bracket to opening bracket

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach, we will use a stack to store the opening brackets
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary, try matching closing brackets with opening brackets sequentially
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details, use a stack to store opening brackets and a map to store closing bracket to opening bracket
